extraction.one_line_summary: SOLER Marc attacked solo from mid-race, bridged 51 seconds to the breakaway, then won stage despite being dropped and catching back multiple times
tactical_patterns: Soler bridged 51 seconds solo after breakaway established, UAE allowed complete freedom suggesting GC squad prioritization shift.
Breakaway established extremely late after prolonged attacks, indicating peloton uncertainty about protecting red jersey with multiple team interests.
Jumbo-Visma gave Moliard 5-minute gap despite Fred Wright only 2 seconds behind, revealing willingness to sacrifice breakaway rider's GC position.

rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Either completely off or dominant with no middle ground, missed Tour time limit by 30 minutes weeks earlier then won here showing extreme volatility
rider_signals.MOLIARD Rudy: positive: New race leader but only 2 seconds ahead suggests vulnerability, team may already dedicate resources elsewhere if told it's temporary
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: negative: Breakaway companion given only 2-second deficit could have taken jersey with proper team support, Bahrain tactical passivity costly
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Team allowed 5+ minute gap to breakaway riders, indicating either comfort with form or disinterest in defending red jersey this early
